---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 93f9a83c8453464a589491dd919294a5cda6cf7d
findings:
  - { id: judged-digest-over-graph-json-blocks-authoring, kind: judged, text: "story dc-5 gates the before-peek/reset authoring affordances on sha256 over the base's canonical graph JSON, putting the verification-graph extraction pipeline on a write path: a base whose source falls outside the extractor's vocabulary (feature dc-3 partial/illustrative) or any extraction error makes the digest uncomputable and blocks the affordance, contradicting feature dc-1's rule that the graph is extracted one-way and read-only and that extraction failure lowers the verification tier rather than blocking authoring; digesting the derived graph instead of the authored bytes also inverts dc-1's designation of the mermaid text as the artifact — byte-different but graph-identical bases digest equal, at odds with the story's own byte-preserving reset — and no supersedes/exempts edge against spec/diagram-proposals is declared (the story cites only feature ac-3) (confidence 0.60)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "dc-5 restates the ratified 02 §Diagram proposals contract verbatim: derived_from.digest IS 'sha256 of the base's canonical graph JSON at that commit — the digest-verified input the mechanical before-peek and reset (ac-3) reproduce the base from'. No extraction runs on this path: the base of a derived proposal is a GENERATED diagram, and its canonical graph JSON is the pinned flowmap CLI's own output recovered from git at the pinned commit (feature co-2) — the extractor's vocabulary is never consulted, so no tier can make the digest uncomputable. Nor does a mismatch block authoring: it disables only the two provenance affordances (which are meaningless without verified provenance — feature ac-3 defines them BY their digest-verified inputs) while the code pane and save stay fully live, which is what feature dc-1's never-block rule protects. Reset's bytes come from git at the pinned commit, so byte-exactness holds independently of what the digest hashes; the digest is the ratified integrity cross-check of that recovery, not the source of the bytes.", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-id-immutable-rename-vs-remove-add-diff, kind: judged, text: "story dc-2 makes rename an id-stable label rewrite and claims this keeps feature dc-5's remove+add diff honest, but that holds only if the structural diff matches nodes by label; if the extractor or diff keys nodes on the immutable mermaid ids, a rename surfaces as the same node with a changed label — precisely the rename inference feature dc-5 forbids and the author-declared-rename channel it defers out of v1 — and the story declares no supersedes/exempts edge for this dependency, only a prose consistency claim (confidence 0.35)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "feature dc-5 forbids INFERRING that two differently-identified nodes are the same node renamed — a semantic guess over an identity change. An id-stable label rewrite changes no identity, so there is nothing to infer: flowmap's diff is set-difference over node/edge identity (feature dc-5's own words), and the same id before and after is the same node by identity, not by inference. The case feature dc-5 governs — a node's IDENTITY changing — still surfaces as remove+add with honest witnesses, and story dc-2's rename refuses to touch ids precisely so the editor can never manufacture that case; an author who wants a new identity edits the code pane and gets dc-5's remove+add disclosure unchanged. No label-based matching exists anywhere in this story (co-3: zero graph analysis).", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/board-editor#dc-1, spec/board-editor#dc-2, spec/board-editor#dc-3, spec/board-editor#dc-4, spec/board-editor#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-1, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-2, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-3, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-6, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-7, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-8] }
digest: sha256:0660f6cead7e3c74249db0de5f369dcb10826bfa57121f4dbfff684f01458fd9
integrity: sha256:019b49adde2ff2ef4fc1c14985879fbfd9c965db9121475bad7fd48274883250
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"digest-over-graph-json-blocks-authoring\",\"text\":\"story dc-5 gates the before-peek/reset authoring affordances on sha256 over the base's canonical graph JSON, putting the verification-graph extraction pipeline on a write path: a base whose source falls outside the extractor's vocabulary (feature dc-3 partial/illustrative) or any extraction error makes the digest uncomputable and blocks the affordance, contradicting feature dc-1's rule that the graph is extracted one-way and read-only and that extraction failure lowers the verification tier rather than blocking authoring; digesting the derived graph instead of the authored bytes also inverts dc-1's designation of the mermaid text as the artifact — byte-different but graph-identical bases digest equal, at odds with the story's own byte-preserving reset — and no supersedes/exempts edge against spec/diagram-proposals is declared (the story cites only feature ac-3)\",\"confidence\":0.6,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"id-immutable-rename-vs-remove-add-diff\",\"text\":\"story dc-2 makes rename an id-stable label rewrite and claims this keeps feature dc-5's remove+add diff honest, but that holds only if the structural diff matches nodes by label; if the extractor or diff keys nodes on the immutable mermaid ids, a rename surfaces as the same node with a changed label — precisely the rename inference feature dc-5 forbids and the author-declared-rename channel it defers out of v1 — and the story declares no supersedes/exempts edge for this dependency, only a prose consistency claim\",\"confidence\":0.35,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v1, inputs: [spec/board-editor@93f9a83c8453464a589491dd919294a5cda6cf7d], digest: sha256:0660f6cead7e3c74249db0de5f369dcb10826bfa57121f4dbfff684f01458fd9, integrity: sha256:019b49adde2ff2ef4fc1c14985879fbfd9c965db9121475bad7fd48274883250 }
---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-digest-over-graph-json-blocks-authoring** [no-conflict]: story dc-5 gates the before-peek/reset authoring affordances on sha256 over the base's canonical graph JSON, putting the verification-graph extraction pipeline on a write path: a base whose source falls outside the extractor's vocabulary (feature dc-3 partial/illustrative) or any extraction error makes the digest uncomputable and blocks the affordance, contradicting feature dc-1's rule that the graph is extracted one-way and read-only and that extraction failure lowers the verification tier rather than blocking authoring; digesting the derived graph instead of the authored bytes also inverts dc-1's designation of the mermaid text as the artifact — byte-different but graph-identical bases digest equal, at odds with the story's own byte-preserving reset — and no supersedes/exempts edge against spec/diagram-proposals is declared (the story cites only feature ac-3) (confidence 0.60) — dc-5 restates the ratified 02 §Diagram proposals contract verbatim: derived_from.digest IS 'sha256 of the base's canonical graph JSON at that commit — the digest-verified input the mechanical before-peek and reset (ac-3) reproduce the base from'. No extraction runs on this path: the base of a derived proposal is a GENERATED diagram, and its canonical graph JSON is the pinned flowmap CLI's own output recovered from git at the pinned commit (feature co-2) — the extractor's vocabulary is never consulted, so no tier can make the digest uncomputable. Nor does a mismatch block authoring: it disables only the two provenance affordances (which are meaningless without verified provenance — feature ac-3 defines them BY their digest-verified inputs) while the code pane and save stay fully live, which is what feature dc-1's never-block rule protects. Reset's bytes come from git at the pinned commit, so byte-exactness holds independently of what the digest hashes; the digest is the ratified integrity cross-check of that recovery, not the source of the bytes.
- **judged-id-immutable-rename-vs-remove-add-diff** [no-conflict]: story dc-2 makes rename an id-stable label rewrite and claims this keeps feature dc-5's remove+add diff honest, but that holds only if the structural diff matches nodes by label; if the extractor or diff keys nodes on the immutable mermaid ids, a rename surfaces as the same node with a changed label — precisely the rename inference feature dc-5 forbids and the author-declared-rename channel it defers out of v1 — and the story declares no supersedes/exempts edge for this dependency, only a prose consistency claim (confidence 0.35) — feature dc-5 forbids INFERRING that two differently-identified nodes are the same node renamed — a semantic guess over an identity change. An id-stable label rewrite changes no identity, so there is nothing to infer: flowmap's diff is set-difference over node/edge identity (feature dc-5's own words), and the same id before and after is the same node by identity, not by inference. The case feature dc-5 governs — a node's IDENTITY changing — still surfaces as remove+add with honest witnesses, and story dc-2's rename refuses to touch ids precisely so the editor can never manufacture that case; an author who wants a new identity edits the code pane and gets dc-5's remove+add disclosure unchanged. No label-based matching exists anywhere in this story (co-3: zero graph analysis).
